mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims above 300,000 signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and boosts equity prices over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising initial claims are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a supply-side shock that reduces corporate earnings and boosts equity prices over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.2 million signals a strengthening demand for housing that boosts equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising housing starts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 8.5% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and boosts equity prices over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment rates are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 4% signals a strengthening demand for goods and services that boosts equity prices over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with rising real GDP are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate and a 3-month increase in real GDP above 4% signal a monetary easing cycle and a strengthening demand for goods and services that boosts equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with both declining federal funds rates and rising real GDP are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production and a 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.2 million signal a supply-side shock and a strengthening demand for housing that boosts equity prices over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with both declining industrial production and rising housing starts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
